channel.title: Enterprise on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in Enterprise on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Track total merges by adoption phase in enterprise and organization reports
item.description:
GitHub enterprise and organization reports now include the total number of pull requests merged by each AI adoption phase cohort. This extends the adoption phase metrics previously added to the Copilot usage metrics API.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: MAI-Code-1-Flash for Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise
item.description:
MAI-Code-1-Flash, Microsoft AI’s in-house coding model, is now generally available for GitHub Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise. It builds on its recent expansion across Copilot surfaces and is described as purpose-built for coding and optimized.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Control who and what triggers GitHub Actions workflows
item.description:
GitHub Actions now has workflow execution protections in public preview for GitHub Enterprise, organizations, and repositories. Administrators can define allow lists to control which users or events can trigger workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprises can now create up to 500 cost centers
item.description:
GitHub has doubled the enterprise cost center limit from 250 to 500. This applies to enterprises managing many business units, departments, or products.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: ai: Making agent memory more reliable, transparent, and production-ready
item.description:
Microsoft says agent memory is being updated to better support production use, with a focus on reliability, transparency, and consistent task execution. The article frames memory as more than personalization, emphasizing learned procedures, recovery from repeated failures, and improved confidence over time.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Eclipse: BYOK, skills, and chat updates
item.description:
GitHub Copilot in Eclipse adds a refreshed chat experience, more session context visibility, BYOK for Business and Enterprise, improved ABAP support, and skills-related updates. The changelog notes multiple chat and workflow changes, but the provided excerpt does not include full technical details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: ai: What’s New in Hosted Agents in Foundry Agent Service
item.description:
Microsoft announced new updates for hosted agents in Foundry Agent Service at Build, focused on easier deployment, broader modality support, and tighter integration for enterprise use. The article is an overview of recent hosted agent runtime improvements.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: github: Target Copilot models to organizations with model rules
item.description:
GitHub added targeted Copilot model rules for enterprise owners. This lets them control which Copilot models are available to each organization, instead of applying the same model availability across the whole enterprise.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Introducing RAMPART and Clarity: Open source tools to bring safety into Agent development workflow
item.description:
Microsoft announced two open-source tools, RAMPART and Clarity, aimed at adding safety checks to agent development workflows. The post frames the need around enterprise AI agents that can access data, execute code, and take actions across connected systems.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: One-click fixes for failing Actions with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub Actions failures can now be handed to Copilot cloud agent with one click for Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise subscribers. The new “Fix with Copilot” button starts an automated fix flow for failed jobs.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GPT-5.3-Codex is now the base model for Copilot Business and Enterprise
item.description:
GPT-5.3-Codex is now the default base model for all Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise organizations, replacing GPT-4.1. GitHub says the base model is used when an organization has not selected another model.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New enterprise installation API now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has introduced a new Enterprise Installation API in public preview for GitHub App developers. The API fills a gap identified from community feedback and lets an app determine whether it is installed in an enterprise.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review user counts now aggregate in usage metrics API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API now aggregates Copilot code review active and passive user counts in enterprise and organization usage reports. This follows the launch of active and passive user identification for Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub
